summary: Decompiled Pikachu's ftpikachu4 unit into C and wired it into the build after the author reported it was DOL matching. The PR replaced most of the corresponding ftpikachu.s text with src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu4.c, expanded Pikachu special-attribute and Fighter state-field declarations needed by the C, and adjusted related prototypes/action table symbol names so the new C functions could be referenced from assembly data.
what_changed: Added new C source file src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu4.c with 654 lines of decompiled Pikachu behavior covering functions from func_80125D80 through func_80127534_file_end.
Removed 1811 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s, leaving data/action-table references and constants that were not part of the newly compiled C text.
Added ftpikachu4.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in TEXT_O_FILES.
Updated Pikachu and Pichu action-state table entries in assembly from lbl_80125ED8-style labels to func_80125ED8-style symbols, matching the C function names now provided by ftpikachu4.c.

Expanded ftPikachuAttributes in ftpikachu.h by replacing broad padding after x58 with typed fields such as x5C, x60, x64, x6C_scale, x7C_scale, xA0 through xD8, and renamed xE0 to xE0_array_start with a TODO noting it appears to be an array of six floats or two Vecs.

Added ftpikachu4-related prototypes in ftpikachu.h, including func_80126084, func_801260E4, func_80126A2C, func_80126AA4, func_80126C0C, func_80126E1C, func_8012744C, func_801274AC, and func_80127534_file_end.

Adjusted Fighter/CollData declarations in fighter.h to expose fields and unions needed by the decompiled code, including CollData wall/Vec fields and Fighter state variables around offsets 0x2344 through 0x2360 with s32/f32/Vec overlays.
Changed include/functions.h prototypes for func_8008239C and func_800D5CB0, and updated ftMarsSpecialHi.c to call func_800D5CB0(gobj, 0, attr->x2C) without passing the Mars attribute pointer.
Updated ftpikachu1.c to use pika_attr->xE0_array_start after the ftPikachuAttributes field rename.
smart_moves: The PR kept still-address-based names for unknown functions while converting action table data to func_* symbols, avoiding premature semantic names and preserving linkage against the newly emitted C functions.
Using Fighter unions for offsets 0x2344, 0x2348, 0x234C, 0x2358, and 0x2360 let the C express multiple observed interpretations of per-action state without forcing a single possibly-wrong type.

The ftPikachuAttributes expansion replaced opaque padding only as far as the decompiled code required, preserving offset-based names and adding a TODO for the uncertain xE0 array/vector interpretation.
The build wiring was minimal: adding ftpikachu4.c.o while leaving the residual ftpikachu.s object in place, a standard incremental decomp pattern for partially migrated fighter files.

The implementation used existing engine helpers such as Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, func_8007D60C, func_8007D7FC, func_80084F3C, ef_Spawn, HSD_Randf, and collision helpers rather than attempting to inline or rename their behavior without evidence.
decomp_lessons: When moving callbacks referenced by assembly data tables into C, update the assembly table entries from local lbl_* labels to global func_* symbols that the linker can resolve from the new C object.
For fighter state variables near the end of Fighter, unions are useful when the same offset is used as u32, s32, f32, callback pointer, or vector storage depending on the action.
Do not over-name character attributes during early matching; offset names like x5C or xE0_array_start plus a focused TODO are safer than speculative semantic names.
Adding or correcting shared prototypes can affect unrelated fighters; this PR's func_800D5CB0 signature change required a Mars callsite update, so global prototype edits should be checked repo-wide.
A PR can start non-matching and still be mergeable once DOL matching is achieved; the author explicitly noted the status change in an issue comment.
naming_conventions: Unknown decompiled functions retained address-derived names such as func_80125D80 and func_80127534_file_end.
Assembly action-table callback labels were normalized from lbl_80125ED8-style symbols to func_80125ED8-style symbols once corresponding C functions existed.
Pikachu special-attribute fields used offset-based names such as x5C, x64, xA4, xB0, and xE0_array_start.
Fighter state fields used offset-plus-role suffixes such as x2340_stateVar1, x2344_stateVar2_s32, x2348_stateVar3_s32, x2358_stateVar7_s32, and x2360_f32.
The suffix _file_end was used for boundary/placeholder functions such as func_80125D28_file_end and func_80127534_file_end.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large block of ftpikachu.s text was deleted after adding equivalent C in ftpikachu4.c, indicating an incremental asm-to-C migration.
Action table data in both ftpikachu.s and ftpichu.s was kept in assembly but retargeted to C function symbols.
Constants formerly present in assembly, such as HALF_PI defined as 1.5707963705062866f, were represented in C where needed for matching.
Stack-shaping artifacts such as unused local arrays appear in several functions, e.g. s32 unused[2], consistent with preserving compiler output for matching.
The decompiled code used exact action state IDs and flags such as 0x161, 0x164, 0x166, 0xC4C5084, and 0xC4C508A rather than semantic enums.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body initially said the DOL was not matching yet; a later issue comment from the author said "pikachu4.c is finished and DOL matching now, ready for merge."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Pikachu func_80125D80 through func_80127534_file_end to semantic names.
Search for later changes to ftPikachuAttributes xE0_array_start to confirm whether it became an array of six floats or two Vecs.
Check follow-up PRs touching Fighter offsets 0x2344 through 0x2360 for improved state-var typing.
Search for func_800D5CB0 signature changes after PR 374 to verify whether this prototype correction was retained.
Look for PRs that decompile remaining ftpikachu.s data or split more Pikachu files into C.
